tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, potassium, and chloride, with low CO₂, suggesting possible renal impairment and mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating significant renal dysfunction, possibly diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
